methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Netrasemi is a Series A-stage semiconductor company founded by N. Jayakumar that has raised ₹107 Cr (~$13M) to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 4 same-stage, same-sector private companies in Chips & Semiconductors (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI), with supplementary reference to 3 public semiconductor comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) that trade at a median 24.4x price-to-sales.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given available data. The median valuation across the 4 private comparables is $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M (Rain AI) to $5.0B (Etched). This method directly reflects current Series A pricing in the semiconductor frontier-tech space. The public-comp anchoring method cannot be applied because Netrasemi's revenue is not on file—a common limitation for early-stage private companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, centered on $1.5B. This implies a post-money multiple of approximately 115x on capital raised. The primary uncertainty is whether Netrasemi's technical position and market traction warrant positioning toward the median or toward the distribution tails; peer valuations span a wide range ($500M–$5.0B), indicating material differentiation across the cohort. Additional data on revenue, burn rate, or differentiated technical capability would refine this range.
